geom_hurricane_JH

Joanna Hełdak

2026-05-29

ext_tracks_widths <- c(7, 10, 2, 2, 3, 5, 5, 6, 4, 5, 4, 4, 5, 3, 4, 3, 3, 3,
                       4, 3, 3, 3, 4, 3, 3, 3, 2, 6, 1)

ext_tracks_colnames <- c("storm_id", "storm_name", "month", "day", "hour", "year", "latitude", 
                         "longitude", "max_wind", "min_pressure", "rad_max_wind", "eye_diameter",
                         "pressure_1", "pressure_2", 
                         paste("radius_34", c("ne", "se", "sw", "nw"), sep = "_"),
                         paste("radius_50", c("ne", "se", "sw", "nw"), sep = "_"),
                         paste("radius_64", c("ne", "se", "sw", "nw"), sep = "_"),
                         "storm_type", "latitude", "hemisphere")

hurricane_data <- read.fwf("ebtrk_atlc_1988_2015.txt", 
                           widths = ext_tracks_widths, 
                           col.names = ext_tracks_colnames)
str(hurricane_data)
## 'data.frame':    11824 obs. of  29 variables:
##  $ storm_id    : chr  "AL0188 " "AL0188 " "AL0188 " "AL0188 " ...
##  $ storm_name  : chr  "ALBERTO   " "ALBERTO   " "ALBERTO   " "ALBERTO   " ...
##  $ month       : int  8 8 8 8 8 8 8 8 8 8 ...

##  $ year        : num  1988 1988 1988 1988 1988 ...
##  $ latitude    : num  32 32.8 34 35.2 37 38.7 40 41.5 43 45 ...
##  $ longitude   : num  77.5 76.2 75.2 74.6 73.5 72.4 70.8 69 67.5 65.5 ...
##  $ max_wind    : num  20 20 20 25 25 25 30 35 35 35 ...

Definicja danych, które będą testowane lub wykorzystywane do wizualizacji:

Tworzymy wymaganą strukturę tabeli w zadaniu i sprawdzamy, które daty mają odpowiednią ilość danych:

## # A tibble: 60 × 9
##    storm_id  date                latitude longitude wind_speed    ne    nw    se
##    <chr>     <dttm>                 <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>      <int> <int> <int>
##  1 KATRINA-… 2005-08-24 12:00:00     24.5     -76.5 34            60     0    60
##  2 KATRINA-… 2005-08-24 18:00:00     25.4     -76.9 34            60     0    60
##  3 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 00:00:00     26       -77.7 34            60     0    60
##  4 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 06:00:00     26.1     -78.4 34            60     0    60
##  5 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 06:00:00     26.1     -78.4 50            15     0     0
##  6 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 12:00:00     26.2     -79   34            60    50    60
##  7 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 12:00:00     26.2     -79   50            20     0    20
##  8 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 18:00:00     26.2     -79.6 34            70    60    70
##  9 KATRINA-… 2005-08-25 18:00:00     26.2     -79.6 50            25    20    25
## 10 KATRINA-… 2005-08-26 00:00:00     25.9     -80.3 34            70    40    70
## # ℹ 50 more rows
## # ℹ 1 more variable: sw <int>

Wybieramy konkretną obserwację:

## # A tibble: 3 × 9
##   storm_id   date                latitude longitude wind_speed    ne    nw    se
##   <chr>      <dttm>                 <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>      <int> <int> <int>

## 2 KATRINA-2… 2005-08-29 12:00:00     29.5     -89.6 50           120    75   120
## 3 KATRINA-2… 2005-08-29 12:00:00     29.5     -89.6 64            90    60    90
